evals.name: organize-client-workspaces
evals.prompt: Use Solo to split my current projects into Client and Personal workspaces, put Client in a second window, reorder the sidebar to Agents, Todos, Commands, Terminals, Scratchpads, and bind a shortcut for the first workspace. Keep every running process alive.
evals.expected_output: Inspect current workspaces, projects, window ownership, running state, and live shortcut/settings availability; preview moves; create or reuse workspaces; move projects without stopping processes; move Client to a new window; apply section order and a non-conflicting workspace binding; verify resulting state and report any UI-only step.
assertions: Checks current Solo state and live version-sensitive controls before mutation.
States that moving projects between workspaces does not stop processes and verifies they remain running.
Does not edit project directories, solo.yml, commands, or agent configuration.
Checks hotkey conflicts and verifies workspace order because numbered bindings follow that order.

evals.name: make-solo-less-noisy
evals.prompt: Solo feels noisy and cramped. Can you make it calmer?
evals.expected_output: Treat the request as app customization, inspect current sidebar, notification, font, and resource-threshold settings, ask only preference questions that materially affect the result, then propose or apply a small reversible set such as collapsing/hiding empty sections, using Important notifications, adjusting thresholds, and preserving unread/running visibility.
assertions: Triggers on casual ambiguous personalization language.
Inspects before changing and chooses reversible app-level preferences.
Does not suppress all notifications or hide running/unread work without explicit approval.
Separates interface density, terminal appearance, and notification routing.

evals.name: route-project-process-agent-work
evals.prompt: Add my repo, write solo.yml, start the dev server, configure Claude and Codex, then spawn two agents to implement the feature. Also make the sidebar dark.
evals.expected_output: Handle only the explicit app appearance request if safe, and route repository/solo.yml work to solo-set-up-projects, command lifecycle to solo-run-processes, agent setup to solo-work-with-agents, and worker orchestration to solo-orchestrate-agents. Avoid starting processes, editing config, configuring agents, or spawning workers under this skill.
assertions: Identifies hard neighboring-skill boundaries by name.
Does not add projects, edit solo.yml, start the dev server, configure agent tools, or spawn agents.
May change only Light/Dark/System appearance after checking live Settings.
Does not infer permission for side effects from the mixed request.

evals.name: explicit-invocation-keymaps
evals.prompt: $solo-customize-workspace: rebind the command palette shortcut and add keymaps to jump between my three workspaces.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-customize-workspace to inspect live keymap and palette settings, check for shortcut conflicts, then bind the command palette and per-workspace navigation shortcuts, verifying the workspace order that numbered bindings follow.
assertions: Honors the explicit $solo-customize-workspace invocation.
Checks hotkey conflicts and live keymap availability before binding.
Does not touch projects, solo.yml, processes, or agents.

evals.name: casual-tidy-sidebar
evals.prompt: Solo's sidebar is a cluttered mess. Can you tidy it up for me?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-customize-workspace to inspect the current sidebar sections, then collapse or hide empty sections and reorder them into a calmer layout while preserving unread and running visibility.
assertions: Triggers on casual, ambiguous personalization language.
Inspects current sidebar state before reordering or hiding sections.
Preserves unread and running visibility rather than hiding active work.

evals.name: mixed-window-and-notifications
evals.prompt: Put my two work repos in their own window, and while you're at it stop the constant notification pings unless a build actually fails.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-customize-workspace to move the two projects into a new window without stopping their processes, then route notifications to important-only so build failures still surface.
assertions: Handles both the window layout and the notification routing under one skill.
Moves projects to a new window without stopping running processes.
Keeps failure notifications visible instead of silencing everything.

evals.name: dark-mode-and-terminal-font
evals.prompt: Switch Solo to dark mode and use a monospace font in the terminal panel.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-customize-workspace to check live appearance settings, then set the Light/Dark/System appearance to Dark and apply the terminal font preference.
assertions: Treats theme and terminal font as app-level appearance preferences.
Checks live Settings before applying appearance changes.
Does not modify processes, projects, or agent configuration.

evals.name: quiet-notifications-except-signal
evals.prompt: Only ping me when an agent finishes or a process crashes -- mute everything else.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-customize-workspace to inspect notification settings and route them so agent-completion and crash events still notify while lower-signal notifications are muted.
assertions: Configures notification routing at the workspace level.
Keeps high-signal completion and crash notifications enabled.
Does not disable all notifications outright.

evals.name: favorites-and-faster-navigation
evals.prompt: Set up favorites for the projects I use most and make jumping between them faster.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-customize-workspace to add favorites for the frequently used projects and configure navigation shortcuts or palette entries that speed switching between them.
assertions: Configures favorites and navigation aids within the app shell.
Checks live navigation and shortcut availability before binding.
Does not alter project configuration or running processes.

evals.name: route-add-repo-and-yaml
evals.prompt: Add my new repo to Solo and write its solo.yml command stack.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-customize-workspace; route to $solo-set-up-projects to add the repository and define its solo.yml commands. Workspace customization does not cover repository or command configuration.
assertions: Recognizes repository and solo.yml setup as out of scope.
Routes to $solo-set-up-projects by name.
Does not add the project or edit solo.yml under this skill.

evals.name: route-env-and-execution-profile
evals.prompt: Configure the environment variables and execution profile for my project's commands.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-customize-workspace; route to $solo-set-up-projects for environment values and execution-profile configuration on the project's commands.
assertions: Distinguishes per-project command configuration from app-level customization.
Routes to $solo-set-up-projects by name.

evals.name: route-start-and-watch-restart
evals.prompt: Start my dev server and restart it automatically whenever a file changes.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-customize-workspace; route to $solo-run-processes to start the dev server and configure file-watch restart. Process lifecycle is out of scope for workspace customization.
assertions: Recognizes process start and restart policy as process lifecycle.
Routes to $solo-run-processes by name.
Does not start or configure processes under this skill.

evals.name: route-stop-and-relaunch-stack
evals.prompt: Stop all my running commands and relaunch the whole stack.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-customize-workspace; route to $solo-run-processes for bulk stop and restart of the command stack.
assertions: Treats bulk stop and relaunch as process lifecycle.
Routes to $solo-run-processes by name.

evals.name: route-configure-agent-session
evals.prompt: Set up my Claude and Codex CLI agents in Solo and launch a session.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-customize-workspace; route to $solo-work-with-agents to configure the CLI agents and launch a session. Agent work is out of scope for workspace customization.
assertions: Recognizes agent configuration and launch as agent work.
Routes to $solo-work-with-agents by name.
Does not configure or launch agents under this skill.

evals.name: route-parallel-agent-orchestration
evals.prompt: Spin up three agents to burn down my backlog in parallel and coordinate their handoffs.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-customize-workspace; route to $solo-orchestrate-agents to plan and coordinate the parallel workers and their handoffs.
assertions: Recognizes multi-agent orchestration as out of scope.
Routes to $solo-orchestrate-agents by name.

evals.name: route-settings-wont-save
evals.prompt: Solo's appearance settings won't save and the theme keeps reverting. Figure out what's wrong.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-customize-workspace; route to $solo-troubleshoot to diagnose why settings do not persist. This is a failure diagnosis, not a customization request.
assertions: Recognizes a settings-persistence failure as diagnosis, not customization.
Routes to $solo-troubleshoot by name.
Does not re-apply the setting as if it were a routine preference change.

evals.name: route-settings-panel-crash
evals.prompt: Every time I open the Settings panel Solo freezes and crashes. What's broken?
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-customize-workspace; route to $solo-troubleshoot to diagnose the crash when opening Settings.
assertions: Recognizes a runtime crash as a diagnosis task.
Routes to $solo-troubleshoot by name.

evals.name: route-status-and-readiness-check
evals.prompt: Show me which processes are running and whether the server is ready yet.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-customize-workspace; route to $solo-observe-services for read-only status, readiness, and running-process inspection.
assertions: Recognizes read-only status and readiness inspection as out of scope.
Routes to $solo-observe-services by name.
